mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls reduce aggregate wage income and consumer spending, which lowers expected corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months with declining PAYEMS over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25%, the Federal Reserve's liquidity provision and forward guidance suppress Treasury yields and discount rates, which forces capital into equities and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During months with FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, the S&P 500 posts negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales reveal a synchronized supply-demand contraction that compresses corporate earnings expectations more severely than either shock in isolation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and RSAFS decline in the same month, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of joint occurrences, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In the aftermath of a sharp labor market shock, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal a durable construction-led recovery that reduces recession risk and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Following months with rising HOUST after UNRATE has risen more than 2 percentage points over the prior 6 months, the S&P 500 posts negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate triggers a rapid repricing of near-term recession probability, causing short-covering and risk-on positioning that generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE declines month-over-month, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a persistent manufacturing contraction that forces broad-based earnings markdowns across cyclical sectors, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: After two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO, the S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of episodes over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25% and nonfarm payrolls rise month-over-month, the joint effect of maximal monetary stimulus and a labor market inflection drives a sharp repricing of growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and PAYEMS increases, the S&P 500 posts negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth print triggers an oversold equity bounce because markets price in peak pessimism and anticipate aggressive fiscal and monetary offsets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Following a negative GDPC1 print, the S&P 500 posts negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of quarters over a 10-year sample.
